title: Beastie Boys Made a Movie. We Made a Beastie Boys Podcast.
format: Sean and Chris Ryan on the Spike Jonze documentary Beastie Boys Story and what the band meant to them, then Sean's interview with Adrock and Mike D about the film, quarantine, The Last Dance, and the movies they grew up on.

segments.heading: Beastie Boys Story
segments.note: Sean and Chris Ryan on the film and the band

films.title: Beastie Boys Story
films.note: The Spike Jonze documentary on Apple TV+, built from the live stage show Adrock and Mike D toured to promote their book. Sean and Chris admire it as a sincere, self-effacing tribute to Adam Yauch, while wishing it had brought in outside voices like Nas, Roman Coppola, the Dust Brothers, or Spike himself.

films.title: The Harder They Come
films.note: Mike D's first example of a rare good dramatized music movie.

films.title: This Is Spinal Tap
films.note: The other exception Mike D names to his rule that dramatized music movies suck.

films.title: Krush Groove
films.note: The Beastie Boys appear in it. Mike D offers it as an example of a not-so-good one; Adam has a soft spot for it anyway.

films.title: The Filth and the Fury
films.note: The Sex Pistols documentary Mike D holds up as the real thing, versus dramatized music movies.

films.title: 24 Hour Party People
films.note: The Manchester/Factory Records film Mike D loves and had put on Adam's homework list. Adam never watched it.

films.title: Footloose
films.note: Mike D's foil for pure nostalgia: a moment frozen in time, unlike the band's ongoing relationship with its fans. Adam admits he's never seen the whole thing.

films.title: Lost Angels
films.note: Adam gets a kick out of seeing his own clip from it turn up in Beastie Boys Story.

films.title: Look Who's Talking
films.note: The reference behind Adam's riff on pitching a talking-baby co-parenting franchise with Mike D.

films.title: Pineapple Express
films.note: One of the not-so-mature comedies Mike D has been binge-watching with his teens in quarantine. Holds up, he says.

films.title: Step Brothers
films.note: Part of Mike D's quarantine teen-comedy rotation. Still holds up, he says.

films.title: Tropic Thunder
films.note: In the rotation too; Mike D notes it couldn't be made today.

films.title: Superbad
films.note: Also on the list, though Mike D found it too uncomfortable to watch with his teenagers.
